goal: Build a working retrieval-augmented generation application in Python that answers questions from a small collection of documents and returns answers grounded in the retrieved source material

user_context.background: Python backend developer with experience building APIs and integrating external services, but without practical experience developing LLM or retrieval-augmented generation applications
user_context.constraints: I prefer project-based, implementation-focused content that builds a complete small RAG application in Python. The project should ingest a modest collection of local documents, split and embed their text, retrieve relevant chunks, send grounded context to a language model, and show the source material used for an answer. Include simple retrieval testing or evaluation. Prefer direct explanations of the underlying RAG steps before introducing frameworks. A lightweight framework or vector store may be used, but avoid framework-only tutorials that hide the ingestion and retrieval process. Avoid Python beginner courses, general prompt-engineering lectures, fine-tuning, advanced autonomous agents, large production architectures, Kubernetes, and complex cloud deployment.
